tips.title: Tips
tips.subtitle: Helpful tips for your baby's nutrition
tips.tip1_content: Start with simple single-ingredient purees. Introduce new vegetables and fruits gradually, waiting 3-5 days between new foods to monitor possible allergic reactions.
tips.tip2_content: Continue breastfeeding or formula feeding. Introduce softer food textures (puree, mashed). Introduce meat (chicken, turkey, veal) and legumes (peas, beans, lentils).
tips.tip3_content: Continue with a more varied diet including fruits, vegetables, meat, legumes, cereals. Eggs can be introduced (start with yolk). Fish can be introduced (carefully monitor baby's reaction).
tips.tip4_content: Make sure your baby sits upright while eating to avoid choking and other complications. Use special high chairs that support your baby's spine.
tips.tip5_content: As your baby grows, gradually increase food texture to encourage chewing and development. Move from smooth purees to coarsely mashed, then to soft pieces.
tips.tip6_content: Choose fruits and vegetables that are in season for maximum freshness and flavor. Seasonal food is richer in nutrients and more budget-friendly.
tips.tip7_content: Offer your baby water between meals, especially in warmer months. Use a small cup instead of a bottle to encourage motor skill development.

banners.banana_1: Bananas are rich in potassium that supports a healthy heart
banners.banana_2: The soft texture of banana is ideal for first bites
banners.banana_3: Bananas provide energy for active babies
banners.banana_4: Easily combined with cereals and yogurt
banners.carrot_1: Carrots are rich in beta-carotene for healthy vision
banners.carrot_2: Babies usually love the sweet taste of carrots
banners.carrot_3: Cooked carrots are easy to digest
banners.carrot_4: An excellent source of fiber for digestion
